channel.title: AI Daily Update
channel.description: Daily high-signal AI intelligence digest -- The Big Three, new tools, hardware and enterprise adoption.

item.title: Anthropic Accidentally Leaks Claude Code Source -- Confirms Claude Mythos Model
item.description: Anthropic's npm release of Claude Code accidentally bundled a source map exposing over 500,000 lines of internal TypeScript -- the second such incident this year. The leak forced Anthropic to confirm its most capable unpublished model, Claude Mythos, described as featuring meaningful advances in reasoning, coding, and cybersecurity. A South Korean developer rebuilt the core architecture in Python within hours, publishing "claw-code" on GitHub before sunrise.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform Launches at GTC 2026 With Seven New Chips
item.description: NVIDIA unveiled the Vera Rubin platform at GTC 2026, delivering a full-stack AI supercomputer with seven chips in full production -- Vera CPU, Rubin GPU, NVLink 6 Switch, ConnectX-9 SuperNIC, BlueField-4 DPU, Spectrum-6 Ethernet switch, and the Groq 3 LPU. The platform promises a 10x reduction in inference token cost and 4x fewer GPUs for MoE model training compared to Blackwell. AWS, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure will deploy Rubin-based instances in H2 2026.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: OpenAI Releases GPT-5.4 Family With Native Computer-Use and 1M Token Context
item.description: OpenAI rolled out GPT-5.4, GPT-5.4 mini, and GPT-5.4 nano to the Chat Completions and Responses APIs. GPT-5.4 is the first general-purpose model with native, state-of-the-art computer-use capabilities, enabling agents to operate desktop software and carry out complex multi-app workflows autonomously. The flagship supports up to 1 million tokens of context. GPT-5.4 mini targets high-volume workloads while nano handles simple high-volume tasks at lowest cost.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: GPT-5.4 Computer-Use API -- Tool Spotlight
item.description: GPT-5.4 introduces native computer-use capabilities directly into OpenAI's APIs. Developers can give agents the ability to observe a screen, click UI elements, type, and execute multi-step workflows across desktop applications without integrating third-party automation layers. Combined with the 1M-token context window, agents can plan, execute, and verify tasks across long operational horizons. This is the first frontier general-purpose model to ship with computer-use baked in, collapsing what previously required separate toolchains into a single API call.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Gemini 3 Deep Think Achieves Gold-Medal Results in Science Olympiads
item.description: Google's Gemini 3 Deep Think upgrade demonstrated gold medal-level performance on the written sections of the 2025 International Physics and Chemistry Olympiads, signaling a step-change in AI scientific reasoning. The specialized reasoning mode is now available across Gemini products and targets research, engineering, and science use cases. DeepMind simultaneously expanded its AI co-scientist access program to all 17 US National Labs.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Microsoft Unifies Copilot Under New Leadership; Suleyman Pivots to Superintelligence
item.description: Microsoft consolidated consumer and commercial Copilot operations under a single team on March 17, elevating Jacob Andreou to EVP of Copilot. Mustafa Suleyman pivots to a five-year mission building frontier superintelligence models. The Azure Copilot migration agent, now in public preview, embeds AI across discovery, assessment, planning, and deployment to dramatically reduce enterprise modernization timelines.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Meta Previews LlamaCon (Apr 29) -- Llama 4 Scout and Maverick Available Now
item.description: Meta's Llama 4 Scout and Maverick are the first open-weight natively multimodal models built on a mixture-of-experts architecture with unprecedented context length. Maverick outperforms GPT-4o and Gemini 2.0 on image-text benchmarks. LlamaCon on April 29 will feature Llama 4 Behemoth, described as one of the smartest LLMs in the world and serving as a teacher model for the Llama 4 family.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Claude Haiku 4.5 Launches -- Near-Frontier Performance at Real-Time Speeds
item.description: Anthropic officially launched Claude Haiku 4.5, its fastest and most intelligent Haiku-class model, designed for real-time applications and cost-sensitive deployments. The model delivers near-frontier performance on reasoning and coding tasks while maintaining low latency. Haiku 4.5 is available immediately via the Anthropic API and claude.ai.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Trump Administration Releases Six-Pronged National AI Policy Framework
item.description: The White House unveiled a comprehensive national AI regulatory framework proposing a single federal standard that preempts state-level fragmentation. Key pillars include child-safety rules for AI products, standardized permitting for AI data centers, and IP protections. The administration is seeking bipartisan Congressional support to codify it into law in 2026. Colorado's AI Act and California's AI Transparency Act are both now in effect.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: OpenAI's $110B Round Leads Unprecedented AI Funding Wave in Early 2026
item.description: OpenAI's February 2026 $110 billion raise remains the largest private venture deal in history. Anthropic followed with a $30 billion Series G at a $380 billion post-money valuation, while xAI announced a $20 billion round in January. Together just three AI companies absorbed 83% of all global venture capital in February 2026, signaling extreme concentration in the AI funding market.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: Legora Closes $550M Series D at $5.55B for Collaborative Legal AI
item.description: Legora, a collaborative AI platform for legal professionals, completed a $550 million Series D, valuing the company at $5.55 billion. The raise reflects surging enterprise demand for AI systems that can handle complex, high-stakes professional workflows. Household robotics startup Sunday also hit unicorn status with a $165M Series B, targeting real-world pilot customer deliveries by late 2026.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update

item.title: RSAC 2026: Agentic AI Security Dominates -- Okta Unveils Agent Kill Switch Framework
item.description: At RSAC 2026, securing agentic AI pipelines was the dominant conversation. Okta unveiled its Secure Agentic Enterprise Framework, featuring an agent registry with identity-federation-based discovery and a universal kill switch propagated via Okta AI Agents. Gartner predicts that over 40% of enterprise applications will embed role-specific AI agents by 2026, making governance infrastructure critical today.
item.dc:creator: AI Daily Update
